# UC-17.4.7 · Cloudflare DDoS Mitigation Event Volume by Attack Vector (`cloudflare:json`)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Resilience &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.*

---

## Description

L3/4 versus L7 attacks need different runbooks. Logpush JSON ties mitigation reasons to colo and rule context.

## Value

Improves incident documentation for regulators and helps justify advanced DDoS SKUs.

## Implementation

Configure Logpush jobs per dataset; route all into a unified `cloudflare:json` sourcetype or use transforms to normalize. Use `spath` for PascalCase fields. Create macros per zone name.

## SPL

```spl
index=waf sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-24h
| where match(_raw,"(?i)ddos|mitigat|dos")
| spath MitigationReason
| spath RuleID
| stats count by MitigationReason, EdgeColoCode
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Web.Web
  by Web.user, Web.url, Web.http_method span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Stacked area (vector over time), geomap (colo).

## Known False Positives

Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.

## References

- [Cloudflare Logpush documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/logpush/)
- [Splunkbase — Cloudflare App for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4501)
